entities.name: vosoritide
entities.context: Vosoritide is a C-type natriuretic peptide analog that was recently approved for improving growth in children with achondroplasia

entities.name: hypochondroplasia
entities.context: Hypochondroplasia is a rare autosomal dominant skeletal dysplasia due to activating variants in FGFR3

entities.name: achondroplasia
entities.context: Vosoritide is a C-type natriuretic peptide analog that was recently approved for improving growth in children with achondroplasia

entities.name: FGFR3
entities.context: rare autosomal dominant skeletal dysplasia due to activating variants in FGFR3

entities.name: C-type natriuretic peptide
entities.context: Vosoritide is a C-type natriuretic peptide analog

entities.name: NCT04219007
entities.context: single-arm, phase 2, open-label trial... registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT04219007)

entities.name: injection site reaction
entities.context: Injection site reactions occurred in 83.3% of participants

entities.name: disproportionate short stature
entities.context: presents with disproportionate short stature with a wide range of clinical severity

entities.name: annualized growth velocity
entities.context: Annualized growth velocity increased by 2.26 standard deviations (SD)

entities.name: height SDS
entities.context: height SDS increased by 0.36 SD during the treatment period versus the observation period

entities.name: enzyme substitution therapy
entities.context: C-type natriuretic peptide analog - acts as CNP to counteract overactive FGFR3 signaling

relations.evidence: Vosoritide is a C-type natriuretic peptide analog that was recently approved for improving growth in children with achondroplasia
Vosoritide is a C-type natriuretic peptide analog; hypochondroplasia is due to activating variants in FGFR3
rare autosomal dominant skeletal dysplasia due to activating variants in FGFR3
Efficacy was similar to what has been reported in children with achondroplasia (FGFR3-related)
Injection site reactions occurred in 83.3% of participants
presents with disproportionate short stature with a wide range of clinical severity
supported by an investigator-initiated grant from BioMarin Pharmaceutical
Annualized growth velocity increased by 2.26 standard deviations (SD)
